channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: How AI can turn restaurant phone lines into profit centers
item.description: Your busiest nights mean the most missed calls. AI answers every one and takes the order.

item.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
item.description: The Trump phone was never a serious phone. Not when it was announced last June, in dodgy renders and with an incoherent spec sheet. Nor when Trump Mobile admitted - just two weeks later - that it wouldn't be made in the US. Not even when the company revealed the final phone, first to me […]

item.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: The tech giant is in breach of the Digital Services Act by focusing on features like infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and the highly personalized recommendation algorithms, the European Commission said.

item.title: 'We need to see the pricing for AI come down': Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora says AI is too expensive — and needs to fall 90% to become affordable
item.description: Demand for AI is there, and performance is no longer an issue – Palo Alto CEO believes pricing needs to come down by as much as 90%.

item.title: Engadget Podcast: Xbox resets with layoffs
item.description: This week on the Engadget Podcast, we dive into Microsoft's brutal Xbox layoffs with Mike Futter of the Virtual Economy Podcast.

item.title: Florida ransomware negotiator convicted for helping ransomware gang extort US companies
item.description: A third ransomware negotiator has been jailed for helping a notorious ransomware group extort American victim companies into paying the hackers.

item.title: Sonos vire ses designers historiques : la décision radicale qui ravive le pire traumatisme de la marque
item.description: Nouveau séisme chez Sonos ? Le fabricant d'enceintes connectées licencie 3 % de ses effectifs et pousse vers la sortie ses cadres historiques du design et de l'expérience utilisateur (UX). Une coupe budgétaire qui inquiète.

item.title: Climatisation mobile ou rafraîchisseur d’air : l’erreur à ne surtout pas faire pendant la canicule
item.description: En pleine canicule, des publicités vantent des « climatiseurs sans tuyau » à 80 euros. Le problème : ce sont des rafraîchisseurs d’air, et ils ne font pas vraiment tomber la température.

item.title: Facebook and Instagram have to dismantle these addictive design features, says EU watchdog
item.description: The European Commission has its crosshairs on features like infinite scrolling. The European Union accused Meta on Friday of breaching its social media law by designing Facebook and Instagram to get users hooked, and demanded it disable “key addictive features” like infinite scrolling.The EU’s executive arm issued a fresh set of charges against Meta Platforms as part of its investigation under the 27-nation bloc’s strict digital rule book known as the Digital Services Act. The sweeping set of regulations from Brussels requires tech platforms to protect internet users under threat of hefty fine...

item.title: Comment empêcher Instagram d’utiliser vos photos pour créer du contenu IA ?
item.description: Dans sa course effrénée à l'IA, Meta autorise désormais Instagram à piocher dans vos photos pour permettre à des tiers de les utiliser pour faire du contenu IA. Voici la démarche pour s'y opposer.

item.title: I’m filling in at The Verge for 6 weeks. Ask me anything!
item.description: Hello my friends! My protocol pals! My computational compadres! I'm David, a technology reporter and cohost of the Waveform Podcast. I'll be hosting a subscriber-only "AMA" today at 11AM PT / 2PM ET. For the next six weeks, I'm filling in for senior reviewer Allison Johnson, who you are all likely very familiar with. That […]

item.title: Empathy for the optimizers
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in for Optimizer here. Bryan Johnson, best known as the man who wants to live forever, has an incurable autoimmune disease. The internet's most […]

item.title: 7 new movies and TV shows to watch on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more this weekend (July 10)
item.description: It's been a lean week for new releases on the world's biggest streaming platforms.

item.title: The funky font on Norway’s World Cup jersey? It’s inspired by Nordic runes
item.description: Nike and the Norwegian Football Federation commissioned a new font called Taakeferd. When Norway’s national soccer team first experimented with kits that used a font inspired by the runic alphabet, an ancient Scandinavian written language used by Vikings, it was too hard to read.

item.title: Saoirse Ronan's new dark comedy Bad Apples finally has a global release date — here's when you can watch the underrated TIFF movie
item.description: Bad Apples is coming to theaters after a festival run. The Saoirse Ronan led movie will be in UK and US theaters from September 18, and we've got a brand new trailer to celebrate the new movie's theatrical release

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 could be the last in the foldable series — and that'd be a real shame, if not a major surprise
item.description: Samsung is reportedly planning to abandon small-screen foldables after the Galaxy Z Flip 8, so we might not get any more clamshell models.

item.title: Why Human+AI collaboration beats AI-only automation
item.description: Despite the hype, AI's greatest value comes from augmenting human judgment, not replacing it.

item.title: ‘The new battle ground for scaling and protecting margins’: AI servers set to consume more power than every conventional data center by 2027
item.description: Gartner recommends infrastructure providers focus on efficiency upgrades to grids and cooling systems.

item.title: 'Sounds like the original launch' — Some fans think the new Black Ops PlayStation ports suffer from input lag but others argue they're the same as the original releases
item.description: Fans are complaining that the new Black Ops ports just don't feel right on PS5 and PS4.

item.title: My top-tested 6 back-to-school ink tank printers for college and university students — because you don't need an 8am assignment printing panic
item.description: I always recommend speedy cartridge-free tank printers for students. They're way cheaper to run with enough ink in the box to last until graduation.

item.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
item.description: Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter out AI content," Mosseri said during an interview on Lenny Rachitsky's podcast. "I think we should let you know […]

item.title: Netflix is expanding its range of content again, and this time it’s chasing YouTube — and I’m starting to question whether it actually cares about the future of its original movies and shows
item.description: Netflix is bringing YouTube-style content to the platform soon — has it stopped caring about its original dramas?

item.title: Volkswagen will dramatically shrink its model lineup and factory footprint
item.description: It follows reports of 100,000 jobs being under threat at the German automaker.

item.title: Boulanger fait pleuvoir les réductions en pleine canicule pour la 3e démarque des soldes : les 18 meilleures offres
item.description: C'est officiel, la 3e démarque des soldes vient de sonner chez Boulanger ! Le commerçant en a encore sous le chapeau et en profite pour casser un peu plus les prix sur une large sélection de produits Tech. Nous avons fouillé le catalogue pour vous dévoiler les offres qui valent vraiment le coup avant la fin.

item.title: Is Microsoft Teams really going to start tracking employee locations?
item.description: Microsoft's new "Workspace Check-in" feature for Teams rolled out last month.

item.title: FIFA's ExpressVPN World Cup sponsorship sparks fury from Spain's La Liga and French broadcasters
item.description: La Liga and top French broadcasters are furious with FIFA for naming ExpressVPN an official 2026 World Cup sponsor. The rightsholders are lashing out at the VPN provider's refusal to implement anti-piracy site blocks.

item.title: Snapdragon Wear Elite : l’architecture de la future puce des Galaxy Watch 9 et Pixel Watch 5 a fuité
item.description: Le Snapdragon Wear Elite, futur cerveau des montres Samsung et Google, vient de passer sur Geekbench. On sait enfin ce qu'il a dans le ventre.

item.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
item.description: A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute nodes in homes […]

item.title: Xavier Niel s’empare de 16 % de Vodafone : ce que cela change pour les abonnés Free
item.description: Le patron de Free met 5,1 milliards d’euros sur la table pour devenir le premier actionnaire de Vodafone. Une opération qui étend encore l’empire télécom de Xavier Niel bien au-delà de la France.

item.title: Dairy Queen store closures: Beloved ice cream chain joins list of retailers to see locations shutter in 2026
item.description: Bad news for Blizzard fans: Alaska now has only one Dairy Queen left. This follows a franchise dispute last year that led to dozens of closures in Texas. Every news cycle seems to bring reports of another fast food chain closing down stores, and this week is no exception.

item.title: Devious phishing campaign hijacks a genuine Meta business feature to send scam emails — as they really do come from Meta's own address
item.description: Meta has put a stop to this attack, after adding additional safeguards.

item.title: China becomes the second country to recover a rocket booster
item.description: China made a breakthrough in its space program with the successful capture of a Long March 10B rocket booster.

item.title: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna are here. See which ones best for you.
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new family of LLMs: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, but they're not all the same.

item.title: Ex-BioWare lead writer would take the series to a 'dark and dangerous' place given the chance, while also going 'back to the basics of what made Dragon Age appeal to so many people in the first place'
item.description: Dragon Age creator and former narrative lead David Gaider doesn't believe the series has a future at EA, but would be interested in working on a new game.

item.title: New York City construction scare highlights the challenges of converting offices into housing
item.description: The recent scare in midtown Manhattan shows the complex engineering behind reuse projects. When two steel columns buckled this week inside the former Pfizer headquarters in midtown Manhattan, the scare prompted evacuations and halted work on one of the nation’s largest office-to-apartment conversions.It also highlighted the complex engineering behind adaptive reuse projects, which have become increasingly popular as officials try to tackle a nationwide housing shortage by transforming offices that have sat underused since the COVID-19 pandemic.The plans call for turning two office buildings — ...

item.title: Xreal’s new AR glasses are way cheaper and almost just right
item.description: I love it when a company challenges itself to make a cheaper version of a beloved product. Xreal's $299 A01 Plus is a stripped-down version of its $449 1S that's light on features but with just enough of the 1S' best qualities. These AR glasses are comfortable, they look good, and the screens are surprisingly […]

item.title: How to watch Sinner vs Djokovic for FREE: live stream Wimbledon 2026 semi-final online from anywhere
item.description: All the ways to watch Jannik Sinner vs Novak Djokovic online and from anywhere for free in a fascinating Wimbledon 2026 semi-final between all-time greats.

item.title: What is the release date for House of the Dragon season 3 episode 4 on HBO Max?
item.description: House of the Dragon season 3 reaches its midpoint with this week's chapter — here's when and where you can watch it.

item.title: HP OmniBook Ultra 14 review: HP's best ultraportable in years
item.description: While fully loaded models are quite pricey, the HP OmniBook Ultra is hard to beat for those in the market for a premium Windows ultraportable.

item.title: Xreal's $299 lightweight a01+ AR glasses are now available to buy
item.description: The latest glasses from Xreal are designed to appeal to gamers and movie-watchers who want a big screen wherever they are.

item.title: Face à la hausse des prix des MacBook, Back Market propose des MacBook reconditionnés dès 464 € [Sponso]
item.description: N’attendez pas le mois de septembre pour changer de PC portable ou investir dans une tablette : Back Market envoie déjà ses meilleures offres de la rentrée 2026 avec un peu d’avance.

item.title: How to watch Wimbledon 2026 online for free
item.description: How to watch Wimbledon for free. Live stream Wimbledon 2026 for free from anywhere in the world.

item.title: Sonys wearable neck air conditioner is already sold out — sign up now for restock alerts
item.description: Sony's Reon Pocket Pro wearable cooling device has officially launched in the U.S. for $259.99, bringing personal neck cooling just in time for summer heatwaves.

item.title: One year later: Looking back at Brennan Quits Dropout
item.description: Brennan Lee Mulligan, Sam Reich, and Vic Michaelis talk about the "Game Changer" video that broke the internet

item.title: Razers new Cinnamoroll headset launches with adorable floppy ears — and its yours for under $140
item.description: Razer’s Kraken Kitty V2 BT Cinnamoroll Edition brings Sanrio’s blue mascot to gaming with floppy ears, Bluetooth connectivity, and a plush design.

item.title: Strava peut maintenant vous aider à trouver votre prochaine compétition de running
item.description: Strava intègre pour la première fois la base de courses de Runna directement dans son application. On peut désormais chercher sa prochaine compétition sans quitter l’app, puis enchaîner sur un plan d’entraînement.

item.title: Don’t fall for the online ads peddling you fake air conditioners ‘developed by NASA engineers’ — UK advertising regulator shares how you can spot and report them
item.description: Scam adverts are trying to sell you fake air conditioners and air coolers, but this is how you can spot them.

item.title: Être payé pour consommer de l’électricité : la promesse folle de Frank Energie (et son piège caché)
item.description: Être payé pour consommer de l’électricité : l’idée peut sembler séduisante pour réaliser des économies. Et depuis quelques mois, ce genre d'offre existe en France. Mais aussi intéressante soit-elle, cette option ne convient pas à tous les consommateurs. Mal maîtrisée, elle peut au contraire entraîner une forte augmentation des factures.

item.title: La dernière DJI Osmo Action 6 avec double écran tactile et ouverture variable revient à prix cassé
item.description: DJI a lancé en fin d'année dernière le modèle Osmo Action 6, qui apporte son lot de nouveautés par rapport à sa prédécesseure. Joybuy prfoite en ce moment des soldes pour casser le prix de cette caméra d'action grâce à un code promo.
